The employee was participating in a sit-in that has been ongoing since 10 September 2019 protesting a planned reduction in wages and increase in work hours. El Fagr News reports the attackers were hired by the owner of the newspaper .

On 16 February 2020, the mother and two sisters of prominent activist Alaa Abdel Fattah, who was arrested in September 2019, organized a protest in front of the Ministry of Justice in Abdeen (Abdeen, Cairo), demanding better treatment for Alaa in prison. The three women were stopped by police who asked about the complaint they had filed and said they would pass it on to officials.

On 11 March 2020, nurses organized a protest in front of Imbabah Fever Hospital in Imbabah (Imbabah, Giza), protesting against the response of the hospital administration towards one of their fellow nurses who contracted coronavirus during the course of her work.
On 6 April 2020, employees of the construction company Orascom staged a protest in Bulaq (Bulaq, Cairo) after administrators would not ease pressure on workers or grant more holidays.
Around 7 April 2020 (between 1 April - 15 April), dozens of civilians gathered to protest in Shubra al Khaymah (Shubra al Khaymah, Kalyoubia) against the burial of people who died of coronavirus.

On 13 September 2020, dozens of residents protested in Al Duwayqah in Manshiyat Nasr (Cairo) to demand compensation after their homes were demolished. Security forces intervened to disperse the protesters and arrested a number of them.

On 20 September 2020, an unknown number of protesters gathered in Al Warraq (Waraq, Giza) to protest against the demolition of homes and buildings for construction projects and to call for the removal of President Sisi from office. Details of the police intervention were not reported except that it occurred.

On 21 September 2020, police intervened in protests and arrestsed at least 4 protesters in Al Warraq (Waraq, Giza), where protesters called for the end of President Sisi's time in office on the week of the anniversary of widespread protests a year prior.

Police allegedly forced 22 confessions.
